---
title: "Preliminary_Analysis"
author: "Annika Richter"
date: "2026-01-18"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
# 2. Analysis
Load Pre-Processed Dataset including the Interleaf measure results

H1 - H3
Fit Models testing for conditions effects for:
- Flow
- Social Presence
- Affect (PANAS)

Each:
1. Linear Mixed Model
2. Variance Test

H5: Likeability
Fit Model 

```{r}
# Load libaries
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(lmerTest)
library(lme4)
```
```{r}
# Load Interleaf data
df <- read.csv("../Data/df_interleaf_measures.csv")
```
```{r}
# Function to extract residuals from model
extract_residuals <- function(model, data, condition_var = "shared_condition_order") {
  data %>%
    mutate(residual = residuals(model))
}
```

```{r}
# Sum Contrasts: Code HAI as -0.5 and HH as 0.5
# What this does: Recode condition order as -0.5 and 0.5, prevents the use of dummy or treatment contrasts, 
# Interpretation: Slope represents raw difference between conditions, intercept each is the grand mean of the dv in the condition
df$shared_condition_order <- as.factor(as.character(df$shared_condition_order))
contrasts(df$shared_condition_order) <- c(-0.5, 0.5)
```
```{r}
# Print Contrast Matrix
contrasts(df$shared_condition_order)
# Check that everything is there and working
attributes(df$shared_condition_order)
```
```{r}
###############################################################################################################
# ANALYSIS
###############################################################################################################
```

```{r}
#######################################  
# H1: Flow analysis: H1 (Flow): Participants will report (a) higher Flow in human-human than in human-AI interaction, and (b) Flow will show greater variance in human-human interaction.
#######################################  

# H1: A Flow model Difference between human-human and human-AI condition
flow_model <- lmer(flow_rating ~ shared_condition_order + chat_number + shared_prompt_order + 
                   (1 + shared_condition_order | participant_ID) + 
                   (1 | dyad_ID),
                   data = df)
summary(flow_model)
```
```{r}
# Variance Test :: Within Participant
# Compares the difference in SD between conditions within participant
df %>%
  group_by(participant_ID, shared_condition_order) %>%
  summarise(sd_flow = sd(flow_rating), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = shared_condition_order, 
              values_from = sd_flow) %>%
  with(t.test(with_ai, without_ai, paired = TRUE))
```

```{r}
#######################################  
# H2: Social Presence analysis: H2 (Flow): Participants will report (a) higher Social Presence in human-human than in human-AI interaction, and (b) Social Presence will show greater variance in human-human interaction.
#######################################  

# H2: A Flow model Difference between human-human and human-AI condition
social_presence_model <- lmer(social_presence_rating ~ shared_condition_order + chat_number + shared_prompt_order + 
                   (1 + shared_condition_order | participant_ID) + 
                   (1 | dyad_ID),
                   data = df)
summary(social_presence_model)
```
```{r}
# Variance :: Within Participant
df %>%
  group_by(participant_ID, shared_condition_order) %>%
  summarise(sd_social_presence = sd(social_presence_rating), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = shared_condition_order, 
              values_from = sd_social_presence) %>%
  with(t.test(with_ai, without_ai, paired = TRUE))
```

```{r}
#######################################  
# H3: PANAS analysis: H2 (PANAS): Participants will report (a) higher positive affect in human-human than in human-AI interaction, and (b) Affect will show greater variance in human-human interaction.
#######################################  

# H3: Affect Difference between human-human and human-AI condition
panas_model <- lmer(panas_rating ~ shared_condition_order + chat_number + shared_prompt_order + 
                   (1 + shared_condition_order | participant_ID) + 
                   (1 | dyad_ID),
                   data = df)
summary(panas_model)
```
```{r}
# Variance :: Within Participant
df %>%
  group_by(participant_ID, shared_condition_order) %>%
  summarise(sd_panas = sd(panas_rating), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = shared_condition_order, 
              values_from = sd_panas) %>%
  with(t.test(with_ai, without_ai, paired = TRUE))
```

```{r}
###############################################################################################################
# PLOTTING
###############################################################################################################
```

```{r}
colnames(df)
```
### Plot Ratings
```{r}
#Canva Colors
teal <- "#0397b2"
damped_teal <- "#a4cfdb"
light_blue <-"#39b6ff"
damped_light_blue <-"#b6e2ff"
blue <- "#1f77b4"

red <- "#ab2f2f"
damped_red <- "#d9a7a8"

green <- "#A4D984FF"
damped_green <- "#e6f4dd"

pink <- "#F588AFFF"
damped_pink <- "#fddee8"

orange <- "orange"
```

```{r}
# Global theme — set once, applies to all plots
theme_set(
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
    theme(
      axis.text        = element_text(size = 18),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = 19, face = "bold"),
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 17),
      legend.title     = element_text(size = 18),
      strip.text       = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
      plot.title       = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
)
```


```{r}
colors <- c(
  "with_ai"    = teal,  
  "without_ai" = damped_light_blue 
)

ggplot(df, aes(
    x = shared_condition_order,
    y = flow_rating,
    fill = shared_condition_order
)) +
  geom_boxplot(linewidth = 0.4, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Flow (Self-Report Rating)"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
df_mean_cond <- df %>%
  group_by(chat_number, shared_condition_order) %>%
  summarise(mean_flow = mean(flow_rating), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(df_mean_cond, aes(x = chat_number, y = mean_flow,
                         color = shared_condition_order)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Chat Number",
    y = "Mean Flow (Self-Report Rating)",
    color = "Condition",
    title = NULL
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = unique(df_mean_cond$chat_number),  
    minor_breaks = NULL                        
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)            
  ) 
```
### Create a Facet Plot Containing Social Presence, Flow and Panas Rating


```{r}
# 1. Prepare data in long format for plotting
df_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(flow_rating, social_presence_rating, panas_rating),
    names_to = "measure",
    values_to = "value"
  )

df_mean_long <- df_long %>%
  group_by(chat_number, shared_condition_order, measure) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(value), .groups = "drop")

# 3. Boxplots (top row)
p_box <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = shared_condition_order, y = value, fill = shared_condition_order)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, linewidth = 0.4, outlier.alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = colors) +
  facet_wrap(~measure, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(c(
               flow_rating = "Flow",
               social_presence_rating = "Social Presence",
               panas_rating = "PANAS"
             ))) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Rating  (Self-Report)") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# 4. Over-time plots (bottom row)
p_time <- ggplot(df_mean_long, aes(x = chat_number, y = mean_value, color = shared_condition_order)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = colors, labels = c("With AI", "Without AI")) +
  facet_wrap(~measure, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = as_labeller(c(
               flow_rating = "Flow",
               social_presence_rating = "Social Presence",
               panas_rating = "PANAS"
             ))) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = unique(df_mean_long$chat_number),
    minor_breaks = NULL             
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),                     
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 10)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Chat Number", y = "Mean Rating", color = "Condition") +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# 5. Combine both rows
combined_plot <- p_box / p_time + 
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1.2)) + 
  plot_annotation(
    title = NULL,
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5))
  )

# Print
combined_plot
```
```{r}
# Save as pdf
ggsave(
  filename = "Flow_Panas,SocialPresence_Between_Conditions.pdf",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 16, 
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300,
  device = pdf()
)
```
